title: Nike, Boston Scientific, And Sysco Are Among Top 10 Large Cap Losers Last Week (March 30-April 2): Are the Others in Your Portfolio?
author: Nabaparna Bhattacharya
description: U.S. stocks closed a shortened week with broad losses as markets observed Good Friday. Major large-cap losers included Nike (down 14.29% after missing Q4 guidance), Boston Scientific (down 9.32% following clinical trial data and analyst downgrade), and Sysco (down 13.56% after announcing Jetro acquisition and analyst downgrades). Other significant decliners included Texas Pacific Land, Venture Global, EQT, Rogers Communications, Kratos Defense, and Antero Resources.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Stock declined 14.29% following disappointing Q3 earnings results and Q4 sales guidance below analyst estimates, with multiple analyst price target reductions
Stock fell 9.32% despite positive clinical trial data (61% reduction in primary endpoint), but Raymond James downgraded from Strong Buy to Outperform and cut price target from $97 to $88
Stock slumped 13.56% following announcement of Jetro Restaurant Depot acquisition and multiple analyst price forecast reductions
Stock decreased 14.30% with no specific catalyst mentioned in the article
Stock fell 15.01% with no specific catalyst mentioned in the article
Stock slumped 11.48% with no specific catalyst mentioned in the article
Stock decreased 9.88% with no specific catalyst mentioned in the article

Stock slumped 9.96% with no specific catalyst mentioned in the article

title: Oil Up 84%, Bitcoin Down 23%: Q1 2026 Turns The Playbook Upside Down
author: Erica Kollmann
description: Q1 2026 marked a dramatic market shift with oil surging 84% while Bitcoin plummeted 23%, signaling a rotation away from mega-cap growth toward real assets and energy. AI infrastructure plays like SanDisk and Lumentum soared on strong demand, while richly valued software and speculative growth stocks faced significant de-rating. The quarter demonstrated that traditional hedges and narratives are no longer reliable, with cash flow and tangible assets becoming increasingly important.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Posted 194% gain driven by AI workloads and increased demand for NAND and edge storage capacity.
Logged 90-100% gains supported by multibillion-dollar AI-related buildout and rising demand for high-speed networking gear.
Energy name that benefited from the oil spike in Q1.
Rode the oil spike and benefited from monetizing West Texas real estate for data center and AI infrastructure.
Spot-exposed Bitcoin vehicle dropped about 23% in Q1, reflecting Bitcoin's poor performance.
Shares effectively cut in half from recent highs as investors questioned business models and durability of growth in an environment favoring cash flows and hard assets.
Shares effectively cut in half from recent highs due to investor concerns about business models and balance-sheet leverage.
Shares effectively cut in half from recent highs as investors questioned business models and durability of growth in a cash flow-focused market.

title: The S&P 500 3 Best-Performing Stocks So Far in 2026
author: Ryan Hasson
description: Despite broad market weakness with the S&P 500 down 2% YTD amid geopolitical tensions, three stocks are significantly outperforming: SanDisk (up 8.10% YTD, benefiting from NAND flash shortage and AI demand), Texas Pacific Land (up 80% YTD, driven by AI data center partnerships), and an unnamed healthcare stock (up over 80% YTD, transitioning beyond COVID focus). Each operates in different sectors, demonstrating selective strength amid market selloff.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Strong outperformance with 8.10% YTD gain, beat Q2 2026 earnings estimates significantly ($6.20 EPS vs $3.31 consensus), 61.2% YoY revenue growth, and positioned to benefit from NAND flash shortage and AI-driven demand for data storage solutions.

Up 80% YTD with strategic AI infrastructure partnership committing $50M to Bolt Data & Energy for 10+ gigawatt data center development. Rising oil prices and water services demand provide additional support. Analysts see 20% additional upside with $639 price target.
Down approximately 2% YTD amid geopolitical tensions in Middle East, capital rotation out of equities, and broad-based selling pressure affecting most sector ETFs trading below support levels.

title: Why Stock-Split Stock Texas Pacific Land Corporation Beat the Market Today
author: Eric Volkman
description: Texas Pacific Land Corporation (TPL) stock rose 0.6% on February 23, 2026, outperforming the broader market decline. KeyBanc analyst Tim Rezvan raised his price target from $350 to $639 per share, citing the company's significant opportunity to develop power generation and data center facilities on its vast Permian Basin land holdings. The analyst believes TPL is well-positioned to capitalize on the booming AI-driven data center industry while maintaining its traditional energy sector revenue.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: KeyBanc analyst raised price target significantly from $350 to $639 per share with an overweight (buy) recommendation. The company is positioned to benefit from high-growth opportunities in data center and power generation facilities driven by AI demand, while maintaining stable energy sector revenue.

title: Moderna, Coinbase, And Global Payments Are Among the Top 10 Large-Cap Gainers Last Week (Feb. 16-Feb. 20): Are the Others in Your Portfolio?
author: Nabaparna Bhattacharya
description: Ten large-cap stocks were top performers last week, with First Majestic Silver leading gains at 25.23% following better-than-expected Q4 results. Other notable gainers include Moderna (19.68%) on FDA seasonal flu vaccine review news, Global Payments (19.52%) with strong Q4 earnings and a $2.6B buyback authorization, and Coinbase (11.32%). Companies like Omnicom, Garmin, Figma, and Pan American Silver also reported strong quarterly results and positive guidance.

Increased 22.52% after reporting better-than-expected Q4 sales and announcing a $5 billion buyback plan
Jumped 18.66% after reporting better-than-expected Q4 results and issuing FY26 guidance above estimates
Increased 21.42% following fourth-quarter results announcement
